title: STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP - Table Will Not Power On
description: Troubleshooting a STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP surgical table that does not power on, focusing on external checks and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the outlet is live using a multimeter or a known working device. Check for tripped circuit breakers or blown fuses in the OR electrical panel. Why it matters: A dead outlet is the most common reason for a table to appear “dead.”

steps.title: Check Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect the table’s power cord for cuts, frays, or loose connections. Ensure the cord is fully seated in the wall outlet and the table’s input port.

steps.title: Inspect Table Power Switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the main power switch on the table is in the ON position. Look for any debris, damage, or stuck switches that could prevent contact.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop or Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify that any emergency stop buttons are released. Confirm any safety interlocks (e.g., foot pedal engagement, bed locking mechanisms) are in the correct position. Why it matters: Many tables disable power when interlocks are active for safety.

steps.title: Test with Alternate Power Outlet
steps.instructions: If possible, move the table to a different verified working outlet. This isolates outlet or OR electrical issues.

steps.title: Observe Table Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for any blinking lights, fault codes, or audible alarms when attempting to power on. Document any behaviors for work order entry and potential manufacturer troubleshooting.

CCR.Complaint: Surgical table will not power on; no lights or response from controls.
CCR.Cause: Verified outlet is live; power cord intact; main switch ON; emergency stops released; table remains unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and scheduled for STERIS repair.

helpfulDetails: Emergency stop and safety interlocks checked
Any indicator lights or alarms recorded
Final device status: Out of Service

title: STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP - Table Movement Functions Inoperative
description: Guide for troubleshooting STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP surgical table when movement functions do not operate despite power being present.

steps.title: Verify Power to the Table
steps.instructions: Confirm the main power switch is on and that the table powers up (indicator lights on). Check that the outlet is live using a known good device or outlet tester. Rationale: Table functions require consistent AC power; insufficient voltage can cause movement failure.

steps.title: Inspect Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions: Check the table’s main fuse or internal circuit breaker (if externally accessible) for blown fuses or tripped breakers. Replace or reset as needed. Rationale: A blown fuse may prevent motors from receiving power even if the table powers on.

steps.title: Examine Foot and Hand Controls
steps.instructions: Confirm connectors are fully seated and free of debris. Inspect for visible damage to cords, plugs, or control housings. Swap with a known working control (if available) to rule out a faulty controller. Rationale: Many movement issues are caused by defective hand or foot controls.

steps.title: Check Motor and Actuator Connections
steps.instructions: Visually inspect external motor wiring and connectors for looseness or damage. Ensure all accessible plugs on actuators are fully seated. Rationale: Loose connections or damaged cables can prevent motor function without affecting power indicators.

steps.title: Test for Locking Features or Safety Interlocks
steps.instructions: Verify that any mechanical locks (e.g., transport locks, lateral lock) are disengaged. Confirm that any safety interlocks (e.g., weight sensors, side rails) are not preventing movement. Rationale: Tables will disable movement when locks or interlocks are active.

steps.title: Observe Indicator Lights During Function Attempt
steps.instructions: Press a movement control and note if lights blink, change color, or remain steady. Rationale: Status LEDs may indicate motor fault or actuator issue, guiding next steps.

CCR.Complaint: Table powers on, but no movements respond to hand or foot controls.
CCR.Cause: Verified AC power is present, outlet functional, controls connected, and safety interlocks disengaged. Motors did not respond; external checks normal.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair/bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights observed during function attempt
Any unusual sounds or lack thereof noted
Final device status: Out of Service, awaiting repair

title: STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP - Hand Control or Pendant Not Responding
description: Guide for troubleshooting STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP surgical table when hand control or pendant does not operate table functions.

steps.title: Ensure Table is Powered On
steps.instructions: Verify the main power switch is in the ON position. Confirm that table indicator lights are illuminated. Why: A hand control cannot operate a table that has no power.

steps.title: Check Pendant Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the cord between the hand control/pendant and the table for visible damage, kinks, or loose connections. Disconnect and reconnect firmly. Why: Loose or damaged connections are the most common cause of unresponsive pendants.

steps.title: Swap Out Hand Control (If Available)
steps.instructions: If another functional pendant exists, swap it to see if the issue follows the pendant or remains with the table. Why: Helps isolate whether the problem is the pendant or the table electronics.

steps.title: Inspect Fuses and Circuit Breakers
steps.instructions: Check any external fuses or breakers specific to the table or hand control circuit. Replace blown fuses with manufacturer-approved equivalents. Why: A blown fuse can prevent the pendant from sending commands.

steps.title: Verify Emergency Stop / Lockouts
steps.instructions: Ensure the table’s emergency stop buttons are not engaged. Confirm no service or lockout switches are activated. Why: Table safety interlocks disable pendant function.

steps.title: Check for Indicator Lights or Error Codes
steps.instructions: Observe if the table or pendant displays any error lights when the pendant is pressed. Document codes for reference or escalation. Why: Codes can indicate specific circuits or control board issues.

CCR.Complaint: Table pendant unresponsive; buttons do not control table.
CCR.Cause: Pendant cord intact, table powered on, fuses intact; emergency stop not engaged; pendant remains unresponsive.
CCR.Resolution: Isolated issue to table electronics. Table removed from service and sent for vendor evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Indicator lights on pendant: Observed / Not observed
Any error codes displayed: List if present
Final device status: Out of service, sent for repair

title: STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP - Hydraulic Fluid Leaks or Slow Movement
description: Guide for troubleshooting slow movement or hydraulic fluid leaks on the STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP surgical table.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety
steps.instructions: Confirm that the table is empty of patients or personnel. Slow movement under load may be normal; troubleshooting should be done unloaded.

steps.title: Check Power and Controls
steps.instructions: Verify the table is powered on and the hand control/foot pedal functions. Confirm indicator lights on pendant respond correctly. Malfunctioning controls can mimic hydraulic issues.

steps.title: Inspect Hydraulic Lines and Cylinder Areas
steps.instructions: Look for visible leaks around the hydraulic cylinder, hoses, and fittings. Wipe areas clean and observe while operating the table to pinpoint leaks. Check for loose fittings or damaged hoses.

steps.title: Check Fluid Levels
steps.instructions: Locate the hydraulic fluid reservoir (refer to external access panel). Verify fluid is at proper level; low fluid can cause slow movement. Only add manufacturer-specified hydraulic fluid; do not mix types.

steps.title: Assess External Obstructions or Binding
steps.instructions: Ensure wheels, bearings, and pivot points are clear of debris. Observe if mechanical linkages move freely. Dirt or foreign objects can slow movement.

steps.title: Operate Table Slowly
steps.instructions: Cycle all table motions one at a time while monitoring speed and fluid behavior. Note any motion that is slower than others or irregular.

steps.title: Document Findings
steps.instructions: Record any leaks, unusual sounds, or slow motions for work order.

CCR.Complaint: Table moves slowly and shows hydraulic fluid under base.
CCR.Cause: Observed minor leak at hydraulic cylinder hose fitting; fluid level slightly below recommended.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service; leak location documented; sent to vendor repair for hydraulic system evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (OOS, sent for repair)

title: STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP - Table Drift or Inability to Hold Position
description: Guide to troubleshoot STERIS AMSCO 3085 SP surgical table drift or inability to hold position, focusing on external factors before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Table is on a Level Surface
steps.instructions: Uneven floor can cause apparent drift. Ensures accurate assessment of hydraulic stability.

steps.title: Check Power Connection
steps.instructions: Confirm main power cord is securely connected and outlet is functional. A table without stable power can intermittently lose hydraulic pressure.

steps.title: Inspect Hand Control / Pendant
steps.instructions: Ensure buttons are not stuck or continuously pressed. Faulty pendant signals may mimic drift.

steps.title: Examine Hydraulic Fluid Level and Leaks
steps.instructions: Look for visible leaks around cylinders or under the table. Low hydraulic fluid can prevent the table from maintaining position.

steps.title: Check for Obstructions
steps.instructions: Ensure nothing is pressing on the table’s moving parts or rails. Objects or linens caught under the table can contribute to drift.

steps.title: Test All Movements Slowly
steps.instructions: Observe if drift occurs in multiple axes (height, tilt, lateral). Note if drift correlates to a specific movement or actuator.

steps.title: Listen for Unusual Sounds
steps.instructions: Hissing, bubbling, or knocking may indicate internal hydraulic issues. Confirms whether problem is hydraulic or mechanical.

CCR.Complaint: Table drifts downward when height is set, cannot hold position.
CCR.Cause: Hydraulic fluid level adequate; no external leaks detected; hand control functioning normally; drift persists in all axes.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent to vendor for hydraulic valve inspection and repair.

helpfulDetails: Drift behavior: slow/jerky, which axes affected

title: STERIS AMSCO 4085 - Table Will Not Power On
description: Guide for troubleshooting a STERIS AMSCO 4085 surgical table that will not power on, focusing on external checks and patient-safe procedures.

steps.title: Verify power source
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is plugged into a known working outlet. Use a voltage tester or another device in the same outlet to ensure power is present. Why: A dead outlet is the most common cause of “no power” issues.

steps.title: Check the main power switch
steps.instructions: Ensure the table’s main power switch is in the ON position. Some tables require the pendant to be docked or the foot switch activated first.

steps.title: Inspect power cord and connections
steps.instructions: Look for visible damage, kinks, or loose connections on the power cord. Ensure connectors are fully seated into both the table and wall outlet.

steps.title: Examine circuit breakers or fuses
steps.instructions: Check any external wall breakers or table-mounted fuses. Replace blown fuses with the correct rating if safe to do so.

steps.title: Check battery (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some AMSCO 4085 tables have a backup battery for movement. Ensure the battery is installed and charged; a completely dead battery may prevent power-on.

steps.title: Attempt power-on from alternate controls
steps.instructions: If the table has both a foot and hand control, try both. A failed pendant can sometimes prevent the system from powering on.

CCR.Complaint: Table will not power on; no lights or response from controls.
CCR.Cause: Outlet confirmed live, power cord and switch verified; problem persisted. Likely internal power supply failure.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled “Out of Service,” sent for repair/bench evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Power cord inspected, no visible damage
Attempted power-on via foot and hand controls

title: STERIS AMSCO 4085 - Table Movement Functions Inoperative
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot a STERIS AMSCO 4085 surgical table when movement functions do not operate despite power being present.

steps.title: Verify Table Power
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is plugged into a live outlet. Check that the main power switch is on. Observe any indicator lights on the table base. Why: Even if the table powers on, insufficient or intermittent power can prevent movement.

steps.title: Inspect Foot and Hand Controls
steps.instructions: Ensure the pendant or hand control is securely connected. Check for visible damage to buttons, wires, or connectors. Swap with a known working pendant if available. Why: Faulty controls or loose connections are common causes of inoperative movements.

steps.title: Check Emergency Stop and Safety Locks
steps.instructions: Confirm the emergency stop button is released. Ensure any safety locks or brakes are disengaged. Why: Engaged safety mechanisms prevent movement to protect patients.

steps.title: Observe Table Base Indicators
steps.instructions: Look for error codes, blinking lights, or alarms on the base. Note any unusual sounds or vibrations. Why: Internal relays may attempt to actuate without completing movement, indicating electrical or hydraulic issues.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cables and Connections
steps.instructions: Verify all cables between table base, control units, and pendant are secure. Look for frayed wires or signs of damage. Why: Intermittent connections can prevent motion despite system power.

CCR.Complaint: Table powers on, but none of the movement functions respond to pendant or foot controls.
CCR.Cause: Pendant and foot controls verified; emergency stop disengaged; power confirmed; table still does not move.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, sent for repair evaluation.

helpfulDetails: Audible relay clicks or unusual sounds
Final device status: Out of Service

title: STERIS AMSCO 4085 - Hand Control or Pendant Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting a STERIS AMSCO 4085 surgical table when the hand control or pendant does not operate the table.

steps.title: Verify Table Power
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is plugged into a live outlet. Check main power switch and indicator lights. Why: A non-powered table will prevent pendant operation, but may appear like a pendant failure.

steps.title: Inspect Pendant Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure the pendant cable is fully seated into the table’s connector. Look for bent pins, debris, or damage on the connector. Why: Loose or damaged connectors are a common cause of unresponsive pendants.

steps.title: Check Pendant Functionality
steps.instructions: Examine indicator lights or display on the pendant. If lights fail to illuminate, try a known working pendant (if available). Why: Determines whether the problem is the pendant itself or the table’s interface.

steps.title: Examine Pendant Cable
steps.instructions: Inspect the cable for kinks, cuts, or exposed wires. Gently flex the cable while pressing buttons to see if intermittent response occurs. Why: Internal wire breaks often prevent pendant operation even if the table is powered.

steps.title: Test Table Movement Without Pendant (if applicable)
steps.instructions: Some tables allow limited manual or foot control overrides. Confirm that the table responds to any alternate control method. Why: Helps isolate whether the issue is table-side electronics or only the pendant.

steps.title: Reset Table Electronics
steps.instructions: Power down the table completely for at least 30 seconds, then restart. Why: Some software or electronic glitches may prevent the pendant from communicating.

CCR.Complaint: Pendant does not operate table; buttons unresponsive and no indicator lights.
CCR.Cause: Pendant cable seated properly, table powered, but pendant fails to communicate with table electronics.
CCR.Resolution: Removed table from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent pendant for bench evaluation/repair.

helpfulDetails: No visible damage to table-side electronics
Table movement tested via alternate methods (if applicable)
Final device status: Out of Service

title: STERIS AMSCO 4085 - Hydraulic Fluid Leaks
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot hydraulic fluid leaks on STERIS AMSCO 4085 surgical tables.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move patient to a backup table if actively in use. Do not operate table while leaks are present; risk of slip or sudden movement exists.

steps.title: Inspect the Environment
steps.instructions: Check for any fluid on the floor around the table. Determine if leak is coming from the table or external sources (spills from IV pumps, cleaning solution, etc.).

steps.title: Check Hydraulic Reservoir Level (External Access Only)
steps.instructions: If accessible, inspect the hydraulic reservoir for obvious cracks or loose fittings. Do not open or disassemble internal components beyond what the manufacturer allows.

steps.title: Examine Visible Hoses, Fittings, and Cylinders
steps.instructions: Look for signs of wetness or weeping around hydraulic lines. Confirm that all fittings appear tight and not corroded or damaged.

steps.title: Operate Table Slowly (If Safe and No Patient Present)
steps.instructions: Observe movement for areas where fluid appears. Listen for hissing or unusual sounds, which may indicate a ruptured seal.

steps.title: Contain and Clean Minor Leaks
steps.instructions: Use absorbent pads or a tray under leak points to prevent slips or contamination. Mark area clearly to avoid accidental contact.

steps.title: If the Problem Persists
steps.instructions: After external inspection, if leak source cannot be resolved, internal hydraulic components (cylinders, seals, pumps) are likely compromised. Remove table from service. Label 'Out of Service – Hydraulic Leak.' Send to STERIS-authorized service or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: OR staff reported hydraulic fluid pooling under the surgical table and slow movement.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed fluid around the base and lift cylinders; hoses and fittings appear intact, but internal seals may be compromised.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for STERIS service evaluation. Area cleaned and absorbent pads placed to contain fluid.

helpfulDetails: Location and severity of visible leaks
Final table status (Out of Service, tagged)

title: STERIS AMSCO 4085 - Table Drift or Position Instability
description: Guide for troubleshooting STERIS AMSCO 4085 surgical table drifting or position instability, focusing on external checks before internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to another stable table if possible. Do not troubleshoot on an active patient to prevent injury.

steps.title: Verify Table is Properly Powered
steps.instructions: Confirm the table is connected to a known good outlet. Check that the main power switch is on and indicator lights are illuminated.

steps.title: Check Hand and Foot Controls
steps.instructions: Inspect for visible damage or loose connections. Swap control pendant or hand control with a known working unit if available.

steps.title: Inspect Hydraulic System Externally
steps.instructions: Look for visible hydraulic fluid leaks around the base, column, or lift mechanisms. Observe if any hoses or fittings appear wet or loose.

steps.title: Examine Table Lock Mechanisms
steps.instructions: Confirm that all position locks or brakes are engaged properly. Some models have mechanical locks that prevent drift when engaged.

steps.title: Test Table Movement Without Load
steps.instructions: With no patient or heavy equipment on the table, raise/lower and tilt slowly. Observe if the table drifts under its own weight.

steps.title: Check for Uneven or Jerky Motion
steps.instructions: Uneven movement may indicate low hydraulic fluid levels or air in the system. Listen for abnormal noises such as hissing or knocking, which may indicate internal leaks.

CCR.Complaint: Table drifts down when set at Trendelenburg, even with controls idle.
CCR.Cause: Observed minor hydraulic fluid seepage at the base and drift during unloaded tests.
CCR.Resolution: Table removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for bench evaluation by STERIS-approved repair.

helpfulDetails: Any audible hissing or jerky movement noted
Hydraulic fluid level observed if accessible externally
Final device status: Out of Service
